home.title: Arabic — Revision
home.intro: Reference for G9 IB MYP Arabic Language Acquisition: alphabet, themed vocabulary, basic grammar, and useful phrases. Arabic is written right-to-left.

extras.title: Arabic — Extras
extras.intro: Topics beyond the G9 syllabus — numerals, verb patterns, dialects, culture.

numerals.title: Arabic numerals (Eastern)
numerals.body: Modern Arabic uses two numeral systems. Western Arabic numerals (0–9) — what English uses, derived from Arabic. Eastern Arabic numerals (٠١٢٣٤٥٦٧٨٩) — used in MSA and many Arab countries. Read left-to-right despite the right-to-left script. 2026 in Eastern: ٢٠٢٦.

verbForms.title: Ten verb forms (awzān)
verbForms.body: Arabic verbs derive from 3-letter roots. Ten patterns (forms I–X) modify the root for different meanings. Form I — basic action (كَتَبَ kataba — he wrote). Form II — intensive/causative (كَتَّبَ kattaba — he made write). Form III — reciprocal (كَاتَبَ kātaba — he corresponded). Form V — reflexive of II. Form VII — passive. Form VIII — reflexive. Form X — request/seek. Learning to recognise the pattern unlocks vocabulary growth.

dialects.title: Dialects vs MSA
items: Modern Standard Arabic (MSA) — used in news, books, formal contexts. What you study in school.
Egyptian — most widely understood dialect due to film and TV.
Levantine — Syria, Lebanon, Jordan, Palestine — soft, often called 'gentle Arabic'.
Gulf — Saudi Arabia, UAE, Qatar — preserves more classical features.
Maghrebi — Morocco, Algeria, Tunisia — heavy Berber and French influence, hardest for others to understand.

culture.title: Cultural insights
culture.body: Arabic is the liturgical language of Islam — over 1.8 billion people connect with it through the Qur'an. The Arab world has 22 countries. Major contributions: algebra (al-jabr), the numeral system (via India), astronomy, medicine, philosophy (Avicenna, Averroes). The Arabic script has inspired calligraphy as a major art form. Common greetings: السلام عليكم (peace be upon you).

literature.title: Classics & poetry
items: One Thousand and One Nights (ألف ليلة وليلة) — frame story including Aladdin, Sinbad, Ali Baba.
Mahmoud Darwish — Palestinian poet, voice of exile.
Naguib Mahfouz — first Arab to win Nobel in Literature (1988). The Cairo Trilogy.
Nizar Qabbani — Syrian poet of love and politics.
Khalil Gibran — Lebanese-American author of 'The Prophet'.
